caption.text:
It’s time to FEEL LIKE YOU again ⚡️

I understand how you’re feeling…

Following an ACL injury playing competitive football, I too was fed up of feeling like I’ve lost my whole identity.

I too was struggling to cope with the constant frustration that comes with a long term injury / pain. 

I too was feeling like I was making zero progress at times.

But, the fortunate thing for me was that..

this came around the time I was studying to get my MSc and become a Physiotherapist. 

Alongside my role within the NHS…

I devoted years to hone down my skills first gained from working in professional football & my coaching degree… 

So I could regain a functional & sporting life I once thought I lost. 

I finally found my identity again, and reignited a love & passion for everything health related. 

Since then,

I’ve helped rehab hundreds of individuals

varying from 1-2-1 & group based settings to RECLAIM THEIR BODY.

Kratos Vitality is my 1-2-1 Online service that I created…

to provide a hybrid approach of Elite Physiotherapy & Online Coaching to individuals who are:

- Struggling with an injury or long-term pain 
- Lacking Performance 
- Want to simply move better

Now, this has been a very successful service to date..

with countless of success stories along the way so far (check out the reviews on google).

So I just wanted to re-introduce this program and pin it on my profile.. 

Numbers for this service are kept on the very low side, to ensure maximal service quality to both my online & in-person clients. 

If you’re interested, or want more information - head to the 🔗 in the bio! 

#physiotherapy #rehab #injuryrehab #injury #injuryprevention #athlete #sport

caption.text:
↓ Knee Stability exercises
__

Knee instability is a silent saboteur, and it’s time to stop it in its tracks!

A stable knee:

💥 Protects ligaments from tears & sprains

💥 Improves efficiency of knee movement

💥 Essential for balance, agility, and stopping the knee from “buckling”

💥 Reduces wear on joints and protects surrounding areas

💥 Crucial for rehab & preventing re-injury 

Now, for exercises to be effective - sets/reps need to be tailored to the individual. 

However, you wouldn’t go far wrong with the majority of these exercises starting off with x2 sets of x8-15 reps. 

Progress where you can! 
__

Enjoy the post team 🫡

caption.text:
↓ How to “Prevent” Shin Splints
__

It’s true that Prehab can’t guarantee you will avoid all injuries - but it does significantly lower your risk by improving resilience and load tolerance.

So here’s 11 exercises to help along with a few reasons as to why Prehab for shin splints is beneficial for you:

1️⃣ Strengthens Lower Leg Muscles

Prehab targets the Tibialis anterior, soleus, and intrinsic foot muscles - reducing overload on the shin bones by improving how force is absorbed with every step.
 
2️⃣ Improves Load Tolerance

Progressive Prehab teaches your shins, calves, and tendons to tolerate impact, so your body adapts to running/jumping stress instead of breaking down.

3️⃣ Corrects Imbalances & Mechanics

Weak ankles, poor foot control, and limited hip mobility can often cause shin splints. 

Prehab aims to fix these imbalances early, improving running mechanics and reducing stress on the tibia.

4️⃣ Enhances Shock Absorption

By improving calf strength, foot arch control, and hip-knee alignment, Prehab makes your whole kinetic chain better at absorbing ground reaction forces → less repetitive stress on the shin.

5️⃣ Reduces Risk of Overuse Injuries

Prehab builds resilience. 

Instead of waiting for pain to force rest, it lowers your risk of shin splints and other overuse issues (like Achilles tendinopathy or stress reactions).
__

When it comes to your body and you’re…

❌ Struggling with your movement? 

❌ Feeling tight & stiff? 

❌ Tired of feeling in pain on simple movements? 

✅ Check out my program - The Mobility Guide. 

Will give you the structure, guidance and accountability you need to see long lasting results! 

Comment: ‘GUIDE’ 

and I will send it over to you with a discount code to save you some pennies. 

#shinsplints #injuryrecovery

caption.text:
↓ How to Prehab your Hips
__

Prehabilitation / Prehab exercises is all about building strength, mobility, and stability before problems arise - reducing the risk of injury and keeping you moving freely in your sport or training.

Here’s 7 hip prehab exercises along with a few reasons why it’s important to include them:

1️⃣ Injury Prevention

Weak or tight hips contribute to groin strains, hip impingement, and even lower back or knee pain. 

Prehab can addresses these weak links before they become issues.

2️⃣ Enhanced Performance

Stronger, more mobile hips = better acceleration, cutting, squatting, hinging, and overall athletic power.

3️⃣ Improved Movement Patterns

Training proper hip mechanics reduces compensations (like overusing your lower back or quads) and builds efficiency in every movement.

4️⃣ Joint Stability & Longevity

Targeting the muscles around the hip and pelvis provides stability, protecting against overuse injuries and helping your joints age better.

5️⃣ Confidence in Movement

Knowing your hips are strong and resilient gives you the freedom to push harder in sport, gym, or daily life - without that nagging tightness or fear of injury.

__

Within a smart performance plan, hip prehab blends seamlessly into your training - often as accessory work after your big lifts.

But here’s the truth: most athletes don’t do enough.

If you want hips that feel mobile, powerful, and “bulletproof”, you NEED to be including hip prehab and strength work weekly.

__

💻 Looking to get back to your best? Interested in working 1-2-1 together, currently have a few spaces open

📈 Tired of feeling tight, stiff, restricted or in pain every time you move?

and I will send you over my latest full body mobility program which is already transforming 1000’s of people EXACTLY like you to get results! 

Hope you enjoy the post 😃

caption.text:
↓ Does your lower back light up every time you train?
__

Here’s the truth:

❌ Sitting itself isn’t “dangerous” or the cause of your back pain.

✅ The real issue is staying in one position for too long without giving your body a chance to move.

When you sit for hours, your hips and pelvis adapt to that static posture. Then when you go to lift, run, or hinge, your lower back often picks up more work than it should.

The fix isn’t avoiding training - it’s preparing your body to move again.

Here’s how:

1️⃣ Restoring Hip Movement

Your hips get most of the movement during squats, hinges, and running. If they feel “stuck” after a day of sitting, your back will take over to create motion.

Drills like 90/90 hip rotations and adductor rockbacks restore freedom of movement at the hips so your spine isn’t forced to do it all.

2️⃣ Build Deep Core Endurance

It’s not about having a “strong core” - it’s about having a core that can endure.

Endurance in your deep stabilisers (think deadbugs, side planks) helps your pelvis and spine share load evenly. That means less strain going through your lower back rep after rep.

3️⃣ Gradually Increase Load

Your back isn’t fragile - it adapts when you load it the right way.

Instead of jumping straight into heavy squats or deadlifts after sitting all day, reintroduce load gradually. Start with controlled tempos, lighter weights, and build up. This teaches your back to tolerate stress again while your hips and core do their job.

⸻

✅ Your back isn’t “broken.”

✅ Sitting isn’t the enemy.

🚀 It’s the lack of movement variety that matters - and the good news is, you can change that with the right approach.
__

💻 have no idea what to do, where to start, how to program it? 

Don’t worry, that’s WHY I created my program The Mobility Guide for you. 

Comment ‘Guide’ & I’ll send the link over to you so you can start next week in the best position possible!

caption.text:
↓ How to unlock stiff hips
__

1️⃣ Hip Flexion 

A fundamental component of many functional movements, including sitting down, standing up & bending forwards. 

Mobile hips with good hip flexion contribute to the efficiency and ease of these everyday activities, let alone athletic movements! 

In sports that involve rapid changes of direction, jumping or high knee movements (think sprinting as an example), hip flexion plays a pivotal role. 

Mobile hips support you to execute these dynamic and explosive movements effectively! 

2️⃣ + 3️⃣ Internal & External Rotation 

With the hip being a ball & socket joint, we need to have access to a wide array of movement variability & options. 

Life is not lived in one plane (forwards or backwards). 

4️⃣ Hip Adduction 

This involves bringing towards the body’s midline (groins / adductors). 

Strengthening hip adductors helps to maintain balance, preventing muscle imbalances that can lead to inefficient movement or increased risk of injury. 

The hip adductor muscles contribute to the stability of the hip joint which is essential for single leg weight-bearing activities. 

5️⃣ Hip Abduction

This involves moving the leg away from the body’s midline (include glute med & min). 

Play a crucial role in stabilising the pelvis and maintaining balance, especially during single-leg activities. 

6️⃣ Hip Extension 

Adequate hip extension allows for powerful strides in running. 

The glutes, especially the Gluteus Maximus, play a significant role in hip extension, generating force to propel the body forward during each step. 

If you are lacking in mobility here and the ability to fully lengthen & contract the glutes - activities that require strong hip extension (jumping & leaping for example) will be negatively impacted. 
__

💻 have no idea what to do, where to start, how to program it? 

Don’t worry, that’s WHY I created my program The Mobility Guide for you. 

Comment ‘Guide’ & I’ll send the link over to you so you can start next week in the best position possible!

caption.text:
I never jump on trends, but this one is relatable! 

Things I do love when it comes to all things gym & training related!

ps… 

Business is currently “businessing” & struggled to find the time to get some valuable content out today 😅

Voiceover content will be back towards the end of this week, and the rest of the week I’ll get some helpful stuff out for you guys!

Also, don’t expect anymore “trending” content - feel like I need to force myself to do the walk of shame through Kings Landing (GoT)

If you know, you know

caption.text:
They’ll tell you you’re too old to start..

too late to change, 

too far gone to get strong again.

But your body doesn’t care about age - it responds to what you do today.

In case you needed some words of encouragement today 🫡

user.full_name: Luke Bartlett | Online Physio & Performance Coach

node.accessibility_caption: Photo by Luke Bartlett | Online Physio & Performance Coach on September 11, 2025. ಪಠ್ಯ ನ ಚಿತ್ರವಾಗಿರಬಹುದು.

caption.text:
↓ How to unlock your stiff back
__

Here’s x5 effective back mobility exercises, along with a bonus one too! 

1️⃣ DB Thoracic Rotations with Adductor Stretch

When it comes to mobility, I often place an emphasis on trying to get the most bang for my buck. 

So here, this exercise aims to improve both hip mobility (adductors / groins) as well as improving how the back rotates. 

To regress, you can either use a resistance band or no weight at all! Especially if you are struggling to hit full ROM. 

Experiment with height of the box / bench to really test your groins! 

2️⃣ Elevated Banded Good Mornings 

Having worked closely with some individuals who feel “jammed” or “locked” with their pelvis if they perform this exercise seated on the floor, the elevation allows us to regress the exercise. 

Please bear in mind, you may have to have a higher plate, use a low step or even a bench! 

Another way to regress, if you feel like you’re unable to bend your back forwards, with this exercise have your legs bent at the knee to release the hamstrings - these could be one element stopping you. 

Make sure to let the band do the work, don’t pull with the arms. 

3️⃣ Half Kneeling Banded Thoracic Rotations against the wall

A great exercise that really helps to improve thoracic mobility, particularly important for those of you who need rotation in your sport: tennis, golf, squash etc. 

Keep the side of the body touching the wall. 

4️⃣ Wall Hip Dips

One of my favourites!! You’ll feel why as soon as you test it out. 

Experiment with the distance away from the wall - further away you stand, the tougher it is. 

Great for stretching the side of your back 

5️⃣ Dowel Thoracic Rotations with Med Ball Squeeze 

Adding in things like a med ball squeeze ensures you aren’t compensating to achieve the movement. 

For example, when individuals try to rotate their back, often you will see the hips / pelvis rotating with, to allow you to achieve the movement. 

As well as increasing adductor engagement as a nice alternative for perhaps an isometric hold exercise for those of you struggling with groin pain. 
__

Really hope you find the post valuable 🫡💪

caption.text:
Injuries strip you down, test your patience, and force you to face your limits. 

But they also show you just how much fight, grit, and resilience you really have

💪

user.full_name: Luke Bartlett | Online Physio & Performance Coach

You're not invincible 2. .You're way more resilient than you thought' ಹೇಳುತ್ತಿದೆ ನ Twitter ಸ್ಕ್ರೀನ್‌ಶಾಟ್ ಆಗಿರಬಹುದು.

caption.text:
↓ How to Bulletproof Your Legs
__

1️⃣ Hip Rotation

Healthy hip rotation gives your joints options.

When it’s limited, your body compensates by dumping stress into your knees and back.

Unlocking rotation = smoother movement + fewer overloads.

2️⃣ Hip Strength

Your hips drive athletic performance.

Hip flexors: sprint mechanics + knee lift. 

Glutes: power, acceleration, and stabilisation. 

Adductors/Abductors: control + balance in every stride and jump

Stronger hips = stronger everything.

3️⃣ Tendon Elasticity

Think of tendons as springs.

The more elastic they are, the more energy they can store and release.

That means more explosiveness, better efficiency, and less strain on your muscles.

4️⃣ Ankle Stability

Stable ankles = confident movement.

They sharpen proprioception, improve landing mechanics, and protect against awkward rolls or uneven ground.

5️⃣ Knee Strength + Power

Strong knees spread force evenly through the joint.

When the muscles around them share the load, your knees become more resilient to heavy training and high-speed demands.

6️⃣ 3D Work

Real life and sport aren’t straight lines.

Training in rotation, diagonals, and lateral planes builds resilience where you actually need it - in unpredictable movement.

__

There’s no single exercise that does it all - but master these principles and your legs will be stronger, more resilient, and ready for anything.
